tags.name: Air Wall
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Wall confirmed by multiple sources. Location: WEST/SOUTHWEST of Gili Air (NOT east — Divessi.com east-side claim appears to be an error; all other operators say southwest/west). oceans5dive.com: wall from 8m to 30m. bluemarlindive.com: 18m avg, 28m max. Depth updated to 28m. Bumphead parrotfish REMOVED — only 1 source (Divessi, which also has wrong location) claims this; no other operator confirms bumpheads at Air Wall. Confirmed: gorgonian sea fans (3 sources: Manta Dive, Trawangan Stingray, Divessi), sea turtles, nudibranchs, pygmy seahorses (Manta Dive), wire coral gobies, orangutan crabs.

tags.name: Bounty
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Vessel was a floating dock/pontoon associated with the former Bounty Resort — NOT a construction pontoon for a jetty. Location: southwest side of Gili Meno (confirmed by majority of operators). Sinking date disputed — 1999, 2001, and 2003 all cited; do not state definitively. Storm cause supported by 2+ sources; bombing claim (Oceans 5 only) not included. Depth: top 6-9m, bottom 17-18m. Confirmed marine life: scorpionfish (4 sources), frogfish (4 sources), drummer fish (3 sources), sweetlips (2 sources), sea turtles (4 sources). Pygmy seahorses: low confidence — only 2 sources (Oceans 5, Gili Divers); hedge as occasionally reported.

tags.name: Deep Turbo
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Confirmed as Advanced pinnacle/seamount (sea mounds/coral mounds rising from sandy bottom). Location: NORTH/NORTHEAST of Gili Trawangan (not specifically in the channel). Depth corrected to 35m — Manta Dive says 18-35m, multiple others agree (previous 30m too conservative). Nitrox strongly recommended. Marine life confirmed: batfish, bumphead parrotfish, spotted eagle rays, white/blacktip reef sharks, pygmy seahorses, green turtles, garden eels, gorgonian sea fans.

tags.name: Frog Fish Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: site_type updated from reef to muck — confirmed as macro/muck-style sandy and rubble site by multiple sources. Frogfish species list (warty, giant, hairy) REMOVED — no source confirms all three at this specific site. Only 'clown frogfish' (Blue Marlin Gili Air) and 'multiple frogfish species' (Gili Air Divers) confirmed. Coordinates flagged: OSM note says 'Location might have to be checked with GPS' — coordinate accuracy uncertain.

tags.name: Halik
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Drift confirmed by multiple operators. bluemarlindive.com: Halik 15-40m range, reef with bommies and gorgonian fans. oceans5dive.com: steep slope with gullies, excellent drift, max 20m shallow section. gilidivers.com: big bommies, baby whitetip reef sharks, turtles, barrel sponges, eagle rays. Depth updated to 40m (bluemarlindive.com deep section). Intermediate for shallow sections; advanced for deep 40m section.

tags.name: Jack Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Location corrected to NORTH/NORTHEAST of Gili Trawangan (NOT west). Confirmed marine life: large schools of big-eye trevallies (Manta Dive), trevally/jacks (Divine Divers, Gili Meno Divers). site_type: drift (confirmed as current/drift-dependent site). Difficulty corrected to Advanced — 24-40m range, strong currents require Advanced certification.

tags.name: Malang
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Also known as Takat Malang. bluemarlindive.com: 20m avg, 40m max, large coral heads with overhangs and swim-throughs; 'massive gorgonian fans hang off the walls'; ~10 major Takats confirmed. trawangandive.com lists as one of top advanced sites. Pygmy seahorse claim REMOVED — confirmed at Simons Reef specifically, not Takat Malang. Gorgonian sea fans: CONFIRMED (Blue Marlin, Next Level Scuba). Site type: pinnacle (large rock/coral Takat formations). Depth 40m confirmed, Advanced difficulty confirmed.

tags.name: Manta Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: site_type corrected from drift to reef — Manta Dive describes as 'gentle slope', 'fringing reef that slopes gradually'; JTG Travel: 'current-free cove'. NOT a drift site. Location: south/southwest of Gili Trawangan (also called Sunset Reef by Manta Dive). Depth corrected to 25m (5-25m range). Difficulty corrected to Beginner/All levels per gentle slope nature. Seasonal mantas confirmed but not reliably — 'can fly by' (Manta Dive). Best approximately April-October but disputed.

tags.name: Meno Corner
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: site_type confirmed as reef (slope with bommies) — NOT pinnacle. Topography: 'gently sloping valley with small coral bommies' (Manta Dive Gili Air). NOTE: 'pinnacle from 9m to 30m' belongs to Turtle Heaven, not Meno Corner. Location: a protected, calm site (different exposure from northeast tip — that's Turtle Heaven). Depth 5-30m slope. Marine life confirmed: green and hawksbill turtles, eagle rays, Spanish mackerel, scorpionfish, lionfish. Difficulty: All levels (calm conditions per manta-dive-giliair.com).

tags.name: Meno Slope
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Location corrected to WEST side of Gili Meno (NOT south) — multiple sources say 'west side' or 'south of Meno Wall' which puts it on the west. Depth corrected to 28m (5-28m range, per PADI and Manta Dive). Special feature: underwater lovers statue at 3m depth confirmed by PADI, Manta Dive, Trawangan Stingray Divers. Marine life: green turtles, blacktip reef sharks, eagle rays, barracuda, bumphead parrotfish, giant frogfish (Manta Dive), blue-ringed octopus. Difficulty: Beginner/All levels (calm conditions).

tags.name: Meno Wall
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Wall confirmed. Location: northwest side of Gili Meno. Depth corrected to 30m (PADI says 30m, Oceans 5 says 25m; previous 22m too conservative). Bamboo sharks: UNVERIFIED — only dpmdiving.com mentions this, not confirmed by Manta Dive, PADI, Oceans 5, Trawangan Dive — removed from description. Pygmy seahorses: confirmed by PADI on fan coral. Night diving: STRONGLY CONFIRMED by 4+ sources (Trawangan Dive, PADI, Blue Marlin, Oceans 5). Bumphead parrotfish: confirmed in open water (Manta Dive). Difficulty: Beginner per 'All Levels' across operators.

tags.name: Mirkos Reef
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Difficulty corrected from Intermediate to Advanced — 35m depth exceeds OW certification limit (30m). No 3-source validation found for this site by name. Remains unvalidated.

tags.name: Shallow Turbo
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: trawangandive.com: Shallow Turbo, all levels, drift dive with Biorocks, turtles, schooling fish, unicorn fish, scorpionfish, ornate ghost pipefish. bluemarlindive.com: Turbo (deep/shallow) 22m avg, 32m max — Shallow Turbo shallow section. oceans5dive.com lists as beginner drift. Site type updated from reef to drift per trawangandive.com.

tags.name: Shark Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Glenn Nusa tugboat wreck CONFIRMED by 3 independent sources: terumbudivers.com, divecentralgili.com, manta-dive-giliair.com. Sunk February/March 2016, ~22-29m long, lies at 28m on sandy bottom between Halik and Shark Point. Requires Advanced OW (30m depth). Difficulty corrected from Intermediate to Advanced — wreck at 28m, canyons to 50m per bluemarlindive.com/trawangandive.com.

tags.name: Simons Reef
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Confirmed as advanced deep reef. Depth corrected to 35m — Manta Dive says 18-35m, Blue Marlin says 40m, most sources agree on 35m as max practical depth. Pygmy seahorses: CONFIRMED here (Manta Dive, Diversion Dive Travel) — keep this claim. Gorgonian sea fans confirmed by multiple sources. Note: pygmy seahorses belong at Simons Reef specifically, NOT Takat Malang (previous entry had them at wrong site).

tags.name: Segaluh
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Site cannot be confirmed in any accessible English-language dive source. OSM source was 'Diveschool Notice Board' (unverifiable). Zero mentions in any dive operator or forum source. All marine life claims in existing description are fabricated generic text. Description should not present any species or conditions as site-specific fact.

tags.name: Teluk NBoro
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Zero mentions under this name in any accessible source. May correspond to Teluk Nara / Magic Pier area but unconfirmed. Seahorse claim (borrowed from Mentigi Bay) STRIPPED — no site-specific source confirms seahorses at Teluk NBoro specifically. All marine life claims should not be presented as site-specific fact.

tags.name: Trawangan Wall
tags.note: source is Diverschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Also listed as Halik Reef by some operators. bluemarlindive.com: Halik 15-40m with gorgonian fans and bommies on NW Gili Trawangan. trawangandive.com: Halik, drift dive with ridges and canyons, 6-40m. oceans5dive.com: steep slope with gullies, excellent drift. Depth updated to 40m (deep section). Wall/drift hybrid confirmed.

tags.name: Lombok Dive
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: FLAGGED AS COMMERCIAL BUSINESS. 'Lombok Dive' is the name of a dive company (lombok-dive.com), not a dive site. This entry is misclassified. Should be removed or treated as invalid. No site-specific content should be presented as factual.

tags.name: Vila Almarick Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Biorock structure confirmed by multiple operators as part of the Gili Trawangan east coast Biorock cluster. bluemarlindive.com: BioRocks 15m avg, 25m max, artificial structures promoting coral growth. gilidivers.com and trawangandive.com confirm Gili Eco Trust Biorock network since 2006. Individual structure name confirmed via OSM data; structure specifics (named for adjacent Vila Almarick resort) plausible.

tags.name: Bale Sampan Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Biorock cluster confirmed. bluemarlindive.com, gilidivers.com, trawangandive.com all reference the Gili Eco Trust east coast Biorock network. Individual structure names from OSM; cluster existence is confirmed.

tags.name: Samba Vilas Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Biorock cluster confirmed. bluemarlindive.com, gilidivers.com, trawangandive.com all reference the Gili Eco Trust east coast Biorock network. Individual structure names from OSM; cluster existence is confirmed.

tags.name: Trawangan Dive Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Trawangan Dive operator confirmed via trawangandive.com. Biorock cluster confirmed by bluemarlindive.com and gilidivers.com. Trawangan Dive is a named Gili Trawangan operator with an active conservation program (Gili Eco Trust).

tags.name: Gili Divers Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Gili Divers operator confirmed at gilidivers.com with dive site listings. Biorock cluster confirmed by bluemarlindive.com and trawangandive.com. Gili Divers is a named Gili Trawangan operator participating in the Biorock program.

tags.name: Turtle Point
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-13: Corrected difficulty from Beginner to Intermediate — 35m depth exceeds OW limits (30m max). Multiple operators (manta-dive.com, trawangandive.com) confirm depth 10-35m, suitable 'for all levels' but 35m section requires Advanced. Intermediate is the appropriate overall rating. Site type corrected from reef to pinnacle (confirmed as underwater hill/pinnacle by manta-dive.com, trawangandive.com).
